title: pearsonia madagascariensis
intro: Pearsonia madagascariensis, also known as the Madagascar pear tree or Bongal, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the island of Madagascar. Its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pearsonia madagascariensis include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from pain and swelling. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in herbal preparations to support overall well-being.
